approval_notes: Owner approved starting F002 slice 3 scoped to the React frontend served and wired into the existing Docker Compose stack, reusing the established api /health seam. Domain data (F003 catalogue/offer/evidence/region/quota/Z0) and provider adapters are explicitly deferred (prompt: plan approved with 'autopilot' on the proposed web-frontend scope).

Technology (Vite + React + TypeScript built to static assets and served by nginx:alpine, wired into Docker Compose) sits within the owner-approved React/Vite frontend toolchain in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md and the future static-host plan. The one base image outside the npm toolchain is nginx:alpine, a tiny ubiquitous multi-arch static server, not a managed-cloud service or app dependency. No new heavyweight dependency or managed-cloud service is introduced.

F002 is an epic; this contract intentionally covers only slice 3 (the web frontend). With slices 1 (api + PostgreSQL) and 2 (worker + scheduler) already merged, this completes the service topology, but F002 remains failing until the FULL epic is verified together by a fresh-context full-epic Level 2 evaluation.
objective: Add apps/web, a Vite + React + TypeScript single-page application built to static assets and served by nginx, wired into the existing Docker Compose stack as the web service with a container healthcheck and depends_on api service_healthy. The app reaches the existing API health endpoint through a same-origin nginx /api/ reverse proxy and renders the live API status/version. The canonical scripts and smoke test are extended to build, test, and verify the web service live. No F003 domain data or provider adapters are added in this increment.
scope: Add a self-contained apps/web Vite + React + TypeScript app: package.json (own deps), vite.config.ts, a separate vitest.config.ts, tsconfig*.json, index.html, src/ sources, an offline vitest unit test with mocked fetch, and its own eslint/prettier config.

Render a minimal FreeTier Atlas landing view with a live API status panel that fetches /api/health and shows ok/version or an error state (real integration through the proxy seam, no fake success path).

Add apps/web/Dockerfile (multi-stage: node:20-alpine build via npm ci -> nginx:1.27-alpine runtime) with a portable NPM_REGISTRY build arg defaulting to the public npm registry, and apps/web/nginx.conf (SPA fallback, /healthz liveness, /api/ reverse proxy to api:8000).

Keep the committed apps/web/package-lock.json pinned to public registry.npmjs.org URLs so it is portable; environments that mirror or block the public registry override the registry via user npmrc (host) or the NPM_REGISTRY build arg (image) without changing the lockfile.

Enable the web service in docker-compose.yml (build context ./apps/web, WEB_PORT:80 host mapping, wget /healthz healthcheck, depends_on api service_healthy, restart unless-stopped) replacing the commented deferred block.

Extend scripts/stack-smoke.ps1 and scripts/stack-smoke.sh with three checks: the web container is healthy, GET / returns 200 HTML containing the SPA root element, and GET /api/health through the web container returns 200 with status ok, in addition to all existing API/database/migration/worker/scheduler checks.

Add WEB_PORT and NPM_REGISTRY (non-secret, public default) to .env.example, ignore apps/web in the root eslint and prettier config, and wire scripts/bootstrap-dev.{ps1,sh} (install apps/web deps) and scripts/test.{ps1,sh} (web unit tests + build, guarded/graceful when node or deps are absent).
Update docs/LOCAL_DEVELOPMENT.md to document the web service, WEB_PORT, the /api proxy, and the registry-override guidance (light touch).
out_of_scope: Catalogue/offer/evidence/region/quota domain model and real API domain routes; the app shows only the health seam (F003+).
Provider adapters, extraction, verification, and Z0 classification (F004+).
Public Z0 hosting, static-host split, or GitHub Pages mirror (F009/F015).
Real product UI (catalogue, adviser, comparison, ZIP generator) and Playwright/e2e browser automation (F006/F008/F012 and a later catalogue-experience slice).
Adding a new runtime, framework, or managed-cloud dependency, or any heavyweight dependency beyond the React/Vite toolchain (+ the nginx:alpine static-server base image).
Merging the pull request, tagging a release, marking F002 passing, or proceeding to the next feature.
acceptance_criteria: docker compose config --services returns exactly postgres, api, worker, scheduler, and web; web defines a healthcheck and depends on api service_healthy.
apps/web builds and passes offline: npm ci then npm run build produces dist/index.html, and npm run lint and npm run test (vitest) pass without network access.
After scripts/stack-up, docker compose ps reports the web container healthy.
GET http://localhost:${WEB_PORT}/ returns 200 and HTML containing the SPA root element.
GET http://localhost:${WEB_PORT}/api/health returns 200 with status=ok, proving the nginx /api/ proxy reaches the api service.
A live run of scripts/stack-smoke.{ps1,sh} asserts web-healthy, web-serving, and proxied /api/health in addition to all existing API/database/migration/worker/scheduler checks, and passes.
Stopping and restarting the stack (stack-down without volumes, then stack-up) leaves the stack healthy and stack-smoke passes again.
scripts/test and scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit remain green; the root Python/Node checks are unaffected and web tests/build pass where deps are present.

No secrets are printed or committed; the secret-scan check remains green; .env.example gains only WEB_PORT and a non-secret public NPM_REGISTRY default; the committed lockfile contains no internal-feed URLs; no unsupported free-tier or Z0 claim is introduced.

A fresh independent Level 2 evaluator records adversarial end-to-end results (compose topology, web build, live serve, proxied API seam, container health, restart, regression, and a negative api-down case) in agent-state/evaluation.json before F002 is considered for any status change.

end_to_end_steps: Re-run scripts/stack-up and scripts/stack-smoke on the branch to confirm the slices 1+2 baseline is green before new work.
Build and test the web app in isolation: npm ci, npm run lint, npm run test, npm run build in apps/web, and confirm dist/index.html is produced.
Run scripts/test and scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit and confirm the suite passes (including the guarded web tests + build).
Run scripts/stack-up, then scripts/stack-smoke, and confirm the web container is healthy, serves the SPA on WEB_PORT, and proxies /api/health to the api service.
Run scripts/stack-down (without volumes) then scripts/stack-up again and confirm stack-smoke still passes.
Stop the api service and confirm the web container keeps serving / while /api/health returns a non-200 through the proxy, demonstrating the seam is real and not mocked.
negative_tests: With the api service stopped, GET /api/health through the web container must return a non-200 (proxy fail-through) while GET / still returns the SPA.
npm run test and npm run build must succeed with no network access (offline), proving the unit test mocks fetch and the build has no runtime API dependency.
The committed package-lock.json must contain no internal-feed hostnames (only public registry.npmjs.org URLs), keeping the repository portable.

risks.data_migration: This slice adds no database migration; it is a stateless static frontend and touches no schema. The existing migrations remain the source of truth.
risks.security_privacy: The frontend ships only static assets; no secrets are embedded at build time. nginx exposes only /, /healthz, and the /api/ proxy. .env.example holds only non-secret names/defaults, and the committed lockfile uses public URLs with no internal infrastructure leaked.
risks.portability: Scripts run on Windows PowerShell 7 and POSIX shells and resolve the repo root from their own path. The image is node:20-alpine + nginx:alpine (multi-arch). The lockfile is public-registry portable; registry mirrors are an opt-in override, not a requirement.
risks.zero_cost_safety: The web frontend runs entirely locally via Docker Compose; no hosting, paid dependency, or free-tier/Z0 claim is introduced. It surfaces only the health seam.
risks.regression: apps/web is self-contained (own tooling); the root eslint/prettier ignore it so F001 root-check semantics are unchanged. Script web steps are guarded and skip gracefully when node/deps are absent, never faking a pass.
risks.supply_chain: npm supply-chain exposure is reduced: dev dependencies are pinned via package-lock.json, transitive nested vite/esbuild are deduped to patched versions via an npm override, vitest is pinned to a fixed release, npm audit reports zero vulnerabilities, and no brand-new (quarantined) package versions are required.

notes: Owner approved the slice-3 scope (React frontend served by nginx and wired into the Compose stack, reusing the api /health seam) in the current session; domain data and adapters are deferred. The F002 feature-ledger acceptance steps are unchanged and F002 will not be marked passing by this increment; the full epic requires a fresh-context full-epic Level 2 evaluation.
